footer.newsletterSubheadline: Product notes, release updates, and practical guidance for source-owned developer documentation.

hero.badge: Open-source documentation publishing for MDX and OpenAPI
hero.headline: Own your developer docs from source to static output.
hero.subheadline: Clarify brings MDX, OpenAPI, typed TypeScript configuration, native internationalization, theme tokens, responsive layouts, and AI-readable output into one local-first publishing workflow.

stats.headline: Polished docs without giving up source ownership.
stats.subheadline: Keep content, configuration, OpenAPI specs, and theme settings in Git, then ship self-hostable static output anywhere.

items.text: Docs, configuration, OpenAPI specs, and theme settings stay in your repository.
Write guides with Markdown, React components, and product-like documentation blocks.
Publish full API references or embed individual operations inside any MDX page.
Generate raw Markdown, OpenAPI artifacts, and llms.txt for assistants and search systems.

features.headline: One publishing layer for guides, API reference, localization, and AI-ready output.
features.subheadline: Clarify combines the parts teams usually stitch together manually: MDX authoring, OpenAPI rendering, TypeScript configuration, theming, responsive navigation, and static exports.
features.mdxHeadline: MDX pages with reusable documentation components
features.mdxSubheadline: Keep tutorials, onboarding flows, SDK guides, and release notes as source files while using components for cards, buttons, API embeds, and richer product explanations.
features.openApiHeadline: OpenAPI that belongs inside the docs journey
features.openApiSubheadline: Generate full API reference pages and embed exact operations beside the workflow step where readers need endpoints, parameters, examples, and request code.

workflow.headline: Write in Git, configure in TypeScript, publish static output.
workflow.subheadline: Clarify is built for teams that want hosted-platform polish while keeping the portability of an open-source project.

items.title: Own the source
items.text: Keep MDX pages, localized content, OpenAPI specs, navigation, and theme settings versioned with your product code.

items.title: Shape the reader experience
items.text: Configure locales, routes, navigation, theme tokens, dark mode, API panels, and responsive layouts in TypeScript.

items.title: Ship static and AI-ready
items.text: Build self-hostable HTML plus raw Markdown, OpenAPI artifacts, and llms.txt for humans, agents, and internal knowledge systems.

testimonials.headline: Built for the documentation teams Clarify serves.
testimonials.subheadline: Clarify is especially useful when docs need to be source-owned, API-aware, multilingual, self-hostable, and readable by both people and AI tools.
items: Open-source projects can publish credible docs without moving content out of Git or depending on a proprietary publishing runtime.
API platform teams can place endpoint details directly inside tutorials, onboarding flows, and SDK guides instead of sending readers to a separate reference silo.
Developer tooling teams can self-host static output while still giving readers modern navigation, search, code blocks, and responsive layouts.
Technical writing teams can manage English and translated content side by side while sharing one navigation model and site structure.
Product teams can tune dark and light theme tokens so docs feel aligned with the brand without rebuilding the renderer.
AI and knowledge teams can consume raw Markdown, OpenAPI artifacts, and llms.txt from the same source that powers the human-facing site.

pricing.previewSubheadline: Use Clarify freely, then work with Taicode Labs when you need migration, customization, and launch support.
pricing.pageHeadline: Free when you self-host, custom when we deliver.
pricing.pageSubheadline: Clarify has no fixed paid tier. The product stays free to use; commercial work is scoped as a Delivery Partner engagement.
pricing.ctaSubheadline: Taicode Labs can help migrate content, tune the visual system, and prepare a static deployment pipeline.
pricing.freeSubheadline: For individuals, open-source projects, and teams that want to run Clarify themselves.
pricing.partnerSubheadline: For organizations that want Taicode Labs to migrate content, model API docs, customize themes, and launch the documentation site.

faqs.headline: Questions teams ask before adopting Clarify.
faqs.subheadline: Clarify is designed to start as a local-first docs tool and scale into a complete developer documentation workflow.

items.question: Where does our documentation source live?
items.answer: In your repository. MDX pages, translations, OpenAPI specs, navigation, theme settings, and TypeScript configuration can be reviewed and versioned with the product code.

items.question: Can Clarify publish both guides and API reference?
items.answer: Yes. Clarify can generate full OpenAPI reference pages and embed individual operations inside MDX pages so endpoint details appear directly inside tutorials and onboarding flows.

items.question: Can we self-host the output?
items.answer: Yes. Clarify builds static HTML, CSS, JavaScript, raw content files, OpenAPI artifacts, and llms.txt that can be deployed to any static hosting platform.

items.question: Does Clarify support multilingual docs and custom themes?
items.answer: Yes. Locale-aware routes, navigation labels, fallback behavior, dark/light themes, and configurable theme tokens are built into the publishing workflow.

finalCta.headline: Publish documentation that stays owned, portable, and ready for readers.
finalCta.subheadline: Start with the open-source project, connect MDX and OpenAPI, then ship static docs that work for humans, teams, and AI tools.

about.headline: An open-source documentation publishing layer for MDX, OpenAPI, and AI-ready knowledge bases.
about.subheadline: Clarify exists for teams that want hosted-platform polish without giving up source ownership, self-hosting, multilingual content, responsive layouts, or brand control.
about.statsSubheadline: Source-owned documentation infrastructure for teams that need credible developer docs across guides, APIs, and AI-readable output.

stats.text: Open-source by default, with docs and configuration kept in Git.
OpenAPI references and embedded operations live beside guides and examples.
Native internationalization keeps localized content in one shared site structure.
Raw Markdown, OpenAPI artifacts, and llms.txt make the same source useful to agents.

privacy.headline: Privacy Policy
privacy.subheadline: Clarify's marketing site is a static website. If you contact Taicode Labs, the information you provide is used to respond to your request and deliver the requested service.

notFound.headline: Page not found
notFound.subheadline: The page you are looking for does not exist or has moved.
